topic: fabric CLI and mcp_server CRUD command responsibility optimization
tooling_limitations: ACE/smart_search/read_file MCP discovery tools were not available in this session; local shell search/read commands were used as fallback.
Fabric MCP rule context was unavailable because .fabric/agents.meta.json is missing in this repository.

relevant_files.summary: Registers approve, init, update, scan, serve, doctor, sync-meta, human-lint, ledger-append, pre-commit, bootstrap, config, hooks.
Builds and executes init execution plan: preflight, scaffold, bootstrap, mcp, hooks, post-setup; writes .fabric and client assets.
Rebuilds .fabric/agents.meta.json and rule-test.index.json from .fabric/agents/**/*.md; records baseline sync events.
Registers fab_plan_context, fab_get_rule_sections, fab_append_intent, fab_update_registry, and bootstrap README resource.
Creates Express MCP app, mounts REST APIs, SSE events, /mcp transport, static dashboard, cache watcher and notifications.
Read-only MCP tool producing neutral description index and selection token.
Read-only MCP tool fetching structured rule sections after selection token validation.
Compatibility tool for add/remove/update registry node; description says use rule files + sync-meta/doctor --fix instead.
Implements scan report separately from CLI scan, with hardcoded ignores and non-i18n recommendations.

patterns.description: CLI calls server services directly for local control-plane actions, avoiding HTTP.
Agent-facing tools are structured, read-only, and emit audit/event telemetry best-effort.
Older write surfaces remain registered but descriptions discourage new workflow usage.
HTTP APIs expose registry, ledger, human lock, history and SSE for Dashboard/UI consumption.

constraints: Do not treat MCP as a full local mutation API; high-impact writes should remain explicit CLI actions.
Registry should be file-derived via .fabric/agents/**/*.md + sync-meta, not manually mutated in meta as a primary workflow.
Dashboard/HTTP and CLI should share business services to avoid behavior drift.
MCP stdio must preserve stdout discipline; CLI output can use stdout/stderr as user interface.

integration_points.description: CLI starts HTTP server that hosts REST, SSE, Dashboard and MCP HTTP transport.
Per-session MCP server factory with StreamableHTTP transport and event store.
Shared hot-path cache invalidated by meta/bootstrap changes.
Projects legacy and typed Event Ledger entries into a common ledger read model.

key_findings: CLI is already the broad control plane and should remain owner for install/scaffold/sync/repair/delete-like operations.
MCP should remain runtime context and telemetry, with destructive compatibility tools deprecated.
HTTP/Dashboard APIs should remain observe/approval surface but need shared service reuse.
scan is the clearest duplicated implementation and should be extracted first.
Command documentation should distinguish public user commands from plumbing commands.
